enrichment.short_description: Udio is an AI music generator that turns text prompts into full songs with vocals, remixing, stems, and studio-grade editing tools.
enrichment.starting_price: $10/month (Standard plan); a free plan is also available
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, New York, USA

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Udio is a generative AI music platform that turns short text prompts into complete songs, including instrumentation, structure, and sung or rapped vocals. It was built by Uncharted Labs, Inc., a startup founded in late 2023 by a team of former Google DeepMind researchers, and it opened to the public in a beta launch in April 2024 backed by a $10 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz.

Since launch, Udio has positioned itself as one of the two leading text-to-music generators, alongside Suno, distinguishing itself with an emphasis on audio fidelity, natural-sounding vocal performances, and editing tools that go beyond one-shot generation, such as remixing, extending, and inpainting existing tracks.

Udio's business is also evolving quickly on the licensing front. In late 2025 it settled copyright litigation with Universal Music Group and later Warner Music Group, agreeing to build a jointly licensed AI music creation and streaming experience expected to roll out through 2026. This shifts Udio from an unlicensed generator toward a catalog-backed platform, though it also means some product terms, such as how outputs can be exported or shared, are actively in transition.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Udio Is For
paragraphs: Independent musicians and songwriters are a core audience, using Udio to sketch demos, generate backing instrumentals, or explore arrangement ideas they can later develop further in a DAW. Its stem export and inpainting tools make it more useful as a sketchpad for real production work than a simple novelty generator.

Content creators, podcasters, and small marketing teams also get a lot of value from Udio, since it can quickly produce original background music, jingles, or ad soundtracks without needing to license stock tracks or hire composers for every project.

Hobbyists and non-musicians who simply want to experiment with songwriting are another natural fit. Because Udio only requires a text prompt and optional lyrics to produce a finished track, people with no formal music training can still create genre-accurate songs for fun, gifts, or social sharing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Professional composers and studios that need frame-accurate scoring, deep mixing control, or guaranteed consistency across long-form projects may find Udio's prompt-driven workflow too unpredictable compared to traditional production tools or sample libraries.

Users who need clear-cut, permanent commercial rights or the ability to freely distribute or upload generated tracks to external streaming platforms should proceed carefully. With Udio's licensing terms and export rules actively changing as part of its label settlements, anyone dependent on stable commercial terms should verify current policy before building a workflow around it.

Developers and teams that want to embed music generation into their own apps or automate bulk generation programmatically should also look elsewhere, since Udio does not offer a public developer API and is designed primarily as a consumer web and mobile app.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At its core, Udio generates full songs from text prompts, letting users specify genre, mood, instrumentation, and tempo, and pairs that with either user-written or AI-assisted lyrics rendered as realistic sung or rapped vocals across a range of styles.

Beyond initial generation, Udio's editing toolkit includes Remix (subtly altering a section), Extend (adding new sections while keeping the existing composition), and audio inpainting, which lets users select and regenerate just a portion of a track, such as a bridge or solo, while leaving the rest untouched. Paying subscribers can also export individual stems for use in DAWs like Ableton Live or Pro Tools.

Account features round out the platform: a monthly credit system with pay-as-you-go top-ups that never expire, voice control for saving and reusing custom vocal styles, style blending to combine multiple reference genres, audio upload for remixing existing clips, and an official iOS app that syncs with the web library (there is no official Android app).

long_form_blocks.heading: The Udio Experience
paragraphs: Using Udio feels closer to a creative sandbox than a traditional software tool: a prompt box for describing the song, tag-style controls for genre and mood, and a scrolling feed of generated tracks that can be liked, remixed, or built on further. The interface is approachable enough for first-time users while still exposing enough manual controls to satisfy more deliberate songwriters.

A social, discovery-driven layer sits alongside the generation tools, with public feeds of other users' creations that can be explored, remixed, or used as style references, which gives Udio a community feel that pure utility-focused competitors sometimes lack.

Overall, reviewers consistently point to Udio's output quality and vocal realism as standout strengths, while noting that the platform is in a period of real flux: its credit-based free tier is fairly restrictive, and its commercial and export terms are being actively renegotiated as part of its licensing deals with major labels, so the experience today may look somewhat different within the next year.

features.title: Text-to-Music Generation
features.description: Generate complete songs from a text prompt describing genre, mood, tempo, and instrumentation.

features.title: AI Vocals and Lyrics
features.description: Produce realistic sung or rapped vocals from user-written or AI-assisted lyrics across many musical styles.

features.title: Remix and Extend
features.description: Subtly alter an existing section with Remix or add new sections to a track with Extend, without disturbing the rest of the composition.

features.title: Audio Inpainting
features.description: Select a specific region of a track, such as a solo or bridge, and regenerate just that part while keeping everything else intact.

features.title: Stem Separation and Export
features.description: Export individual instrument and vocal stems for further work in professional DAWs like Ableton Live or Pro Tools.

features.title: Voice Control
features.description: Save, reuse, and blend custom vocal styles across multiple generations on paid plans.

features.title: Style Blending
features.description: Combine references from multiple genres or styles into a single generated track.

features.title: Audio Upload
features.description: Upload existing audio clips to remix or extend them with Udio's generation engine.

features.title: Credit-Based Plans with Pay-As-You-Go Top-Ups
features.description: Monthly credit allotments across Free, Standard, and Pro tiers, plus optional top-up credit packs that never expire.

features.title: Official iOS App
features.description: A native iPhone app that syncs generation, editing, and library features with the web platform.

pros: High audio fidelity with realistic, natural-sounding AI vocals across a wide range of genres
Editing tools like remix, extend, and inpainting go well beyond one-shot generation
Stem export supports integrating generated tracks into professional production workflows
Usable free tier lets new users test quality before subscribing
Increasingly backed by licensed catalog deals with major labels, reducing legal uncertainty over time
cons: Ongoing transition to a licensed, label-backed platform means export and commercial-use terms are actively changing
Monthly credits do not roll over, so unused generations are lost at each billing cycle
Free tier is tightly capped, limiting both daily credits and full-length song output
No public developer API, so it can't be embedded directly into third-party apps or automated pipelines
No official Android app, limiting mobile access for non-iPhone users

faqs.question: Is Udio free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Udio offers a free plan with a limited monthly and daily credit allowance, capped at a small number of full-length songs per day. Paid Standard and Pro plans remove the daily cap and add higher monthly credit allotments plus additional editing features.

faqs.question: Can I download or export songs made with Udio?
faqs.answer: Export and stem download availability has depended on plan tier, and Udio's commercial and distribution terms are actively evolving as part of its 2025 licensing settlements with major record labels, so users should check the current terms on udio.com before relying on a specific workflow.

faqs.question: Who owns the rights to music generated with Udio?
faqs.answer: Rights and commercial usage terms vary by plan and are subject to Udio's evolving licensing agreements with music labels. Users should review Udio's current terms of service for the specifics that apply to their subscription tier.

faqs.question: Does Udio have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Udio has an official iOS app that syncs with the web platform's library and subscription. There is no official Android app; any Udio-branded apps on the Google Play Store are unauthorized.

faqs.question: How is Udio different from Suno?
faqs.answer: Both are leading text-to-music AI generators with similar prompt-based workflows. Udio is often noted for its remix, extend, and inpainting editing tools and stem export, while the two platforms are frequently compared head-to-head for audio quality and vocal realism.

use_cases.title: Songwriting demos and backing tracks
use_cases.description: Musicians use Udio to quickly sketch song ideas, generate instrumental backing tracks, or explore arrangement variations before refining them further in a DAW.

use_cases.title: Original background music and jingles
use_cases.description: Creators generate royalty-conscious background music, intros, and jingles for videos, podcasts, and streams without licensing stock tracks.

use_cases.title: Custom audio for ads and campaigns
use_cases.description: Small marketing and agency teams produce original branded music beds and jingles for advertising campaigns quickly and at low cost.

use_cases.title: Casual music experimentation
use_cases.description: Non-musicians create genre-accurate songs for fun, gifts, or social sharing using only text prompts and optional lyrics.

features: Up to 100 credits per month (10 credits per day)
Up to 3 full-length songs per day
Lyrics editing and full voice control
Good for irregular or occasional use

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Udio review: pricing, features, and use cases for the AI music generator that creates full songs with vocals from text prompts.
